Notes: Most popular flexible linker. (GGGGS)x3 repeat. Polyglycine backbone minimizes steric interference.
(GGGGS)x2 shorter variant. Less separation between domains.
(GGGGS)x4 longer variant. Greater interdomain distance and flexibility.
Shorter GGS repeat. More compact than GGGGS. Good for close-proximity fusions.
(EAAAK)x3. Glu-Ala4-Lys repeat. Alpha-helical. Reduces flexibility vs GGGGS. Good for independent domain function.
(PG)x6. Proline ring restricts phi dihedral. Semi-rigid extended conformation.
(SGGGS)x4. Hydroxyl groups add H-bonding capacity. Polar and soluble.
GGGGS-AAAA hybrid. Combines flexibility (GGGGS) with moderate structure (Ala-helix).
(AG)x13 repeat. Alanine-glycine alternation. Compact and simple flexible linker.
(EAAAK)x4 longer helical linker. Maximum domain separation with rigid alpha-helical structure.
